segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
简为什么把约翰的钱包给“煮”了？

segments.text: Has it ever happened to you?

translations.zh-Hans: 这种事发生在你身上过吗？

segments.text: Have you ever put your trousers in the washing machine, and then remembered there was a large banknote in your back pocket?

translations.zh-Hans: 你是否曾把裤子放进洗衣机，随后才想起后袋里还有一张大额钞票？

segments.text: When you rescued your trousers, did you find the note was whiter than white?

translations.zh-Hans: 当你抢救出裤子时，是否发现那张钞票洗得白得不能再白？

segments.text: People who live in Britain needn't despair when they make mistakes like this.

translations.zh-Hans: 英国人犯了这种错误时不必绝望。

segments.text: And a lot of people do.
Fortunately for them, the Bank of England has a team called Mutilated Ladies, which deals with claims from people who fed their money to a machine or to their dog.

translations.zh-Hans: 幸运的是，英格兰银行有一支叫作“残钞鉴别组”的小组，专门处理人们把钱塞进机器或喂给狗后提出的赔偿申请。

segments.text: it seems, love to chew up money.

translations.zh-Hans: 狗似乎很喜欢把钱嚼碎。

segments.text: A recent case concerns Jane Butland, whose fiancée, John, runs a successful furniture business.

translations.zh-Hans: 最近的一起案例与简·巴特兰有关，她的未婚夫约翰经营着一家生意兴隆的家具店。

segments.text: John had a very good day and put his wallet containing 3,000 pounds into the microwave oven for safekeeping.

translations.zh-Hans: 约翰那天生意很好，便把装有3,000英镑的钱包放进微波炉里妥善保管。

segments.text: Then he and Jane went horse-riding.

translations.zh-Hans: 随后他和简一起去骑马。

segments.text: When they got home, Jane cooked their dinner in the microwave oven, and without realizing it, cooked her fiancée's wallet as well, imagined their dismay when they found a beautifully cooked wallet and notes

translations.zh-Hans: 回家后，简用微波炉做晚饭，无意中也把未婚夫的钱包烤了。可以想象，当他们发现一个烤得恰到好处的钱包以及
已经化为灰烬的钞票时，是多么沮丧。

segments.text: John went to see his bank manager, who sent the remains of wallet and the money to the special department of the Bank of England in Newcastle, the Mutilated Ladies.

translations.zh-Hans: 约翰去找银行经理，经理把钱包和钞票的残片寄给了英格兰银行设在纽卡斯尔的专门部门——“残钞鉴别组”。

segments.text: They examined the remains, and John got all his money back.

translations.zh-Hans: 她们检查了残片，约翰拿回了全部钱款。

segments.text: So long as there's something to identify, we will give people their money back, said a spokeswoman for the bank.

translations.zh-Hans: “只要还有可以辨认的东西，我们就会把钱退还给人们，”银行的一位女发言人说。

segments.text: Last year, we paid 1.5 million pounds on 21,000 claims.

translations.zh-Hans: “去年，我们为21,000项索赔支付了150万英镑。”
